title: Flask-SQLAlchemy
content:
### Installation

```bash
pip install flask-sqlalchemy
```

### Configuration

```python
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config["SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI"] = "sqlite:///site.db"
app.config["SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS"] = False

db = SQLAlchemy(app)
```

### Defining Models

```python
class User(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    posts = db.relationship("Post", backref="author", lazy=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User {self.username}>"

class Post(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)
    content = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
```

### Column Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `db.Integer` | Integer |
| `db.String(n)` | String with max length |
| `db.Text` | Text without max length |
| `db.Float` | Floating point |
| `db.Boolean` | Boolean |
| `db.DateTime` | Date and time |
| `db.Date` | Date only |
| `db.JSON` | JSON data |

### CRUD Operations

**Create:**
```python
user = User(username="alice", email="alice@example.com")
db.session.add(user)
db.session.commit()
```

**Read:**
```python
users = User.query.all()
user = User.query.get(1)
user = User.query.filter_by(username="alice").first()
users = User.query.filter(User.email.endswith("@example.com")).all()
```

**Update:**
```python
user = User.query.get(1)
user.email = "newemail@example.com"
db.session.commit()
```

**Delete:**
```python
user = User.query.get(1)
db.session.delete(user)
db.session.commit()
```

### Relationships

- **One-to-Many**: `db.relationship()` on the parent, `db.ForeignKey()` on the child
- **Backref**: The `backref` parameter adds a reverse reference (e.g., `post.author`)
- **Lazy loading**: `lazy=True` loads related data only when accessed
- **Eager loading**: `lazy="joined"` or `lazy="subquery"` loads with the main query
codeExample:
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from datetime import datetime

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config["SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI"] = "sqlite:///blog.db"
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class User(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    posts = db.relationship("Post", backref="author", lazy=True)

class Post(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)
    content = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("user.id"), nullable=False)

with app.app_context():
    db.create_all()

quiz.question: Which method commits changes to the database in Flask-SQLAlchemy?
What does db.ForeignKey() define in a model?
options: A column that references another table's primary key
